channel.title: Maximalistas
channel.description: Noticias y comentarios maximalistas en español

item.title: In the US, the ESOP becomes the default form of business succession
item.description: The growing support in Washington and the private sector for Employee Stock Ownership Plans (ESOPs) as a business succession alternative indicates an institutionalization and validation of employee stock ownership mechanisms in the US. This news is significant because it shows a paradigm shift where ESOPs, a form of employee ownership, are gaining legislative and corporate traction as a structural solution to the business succession crisis.

item.title: Spain since 2018 in a graph
item.description: Spain between 2018 and 2025. We are experiencing the worst possible evolution: work is devalued (real wages are falling) but labor costs for companies are increasing (so competition for talent among them does not generate more job offers or raise wages).

item.title: Want to improve everyday life in your community? Create an employee-owned fund
item.description: The article argues that employee ownership funds in the US are a significant way to generate a positive impact on daily life, proposing the adoption of ownership models where employees have a direct stake. This highlights the social value and direct impact on well-being as a growing driver for employee ownership, beyond purely financial or business succession benefits. Promoting these funds aims to institutionalize or scale up the employee ownership model as a socially responsible and effective solution.

item.title: Mead Lumber: 25 years of worker ownership in the US
item.description:
“When Mead Lumber embarked on its journey toward employee ownership 25 years ago, no one could have predicted the impact it would have on our people and our future,” said Dave Anderson, president and CEO of Mead Lumber. “We are proud that more than 30 of those first employee-owners are still with us today. Their commitment, along with that of our new employee-owners, has forged a culture of trust, service, and shared success. Our story demonstrates that when employees are truly invested in the business, everyone wins.”

This milestone will be celebrated at all 53 locations in nine states, where employees will gather for events, team meals, and to hear from the more than 30 current employees who were part of the first group of employee owners 25 years ago. Employee ownership has brought long-term benefits to Mead Lumber and its people.

item.title: Fiscal policy in the US is geared towards encouraging workers to become homeowners
item.description: In the US, strategic tax tools to facilitate business succession in local businesses are being debated and approved at both the state and federal levels. The connection between tax benefits and business continuity through employee ownership is the result of an institutional shift toward adopting EOT/WBO models as the preferred solution for business succession in SMEs.

item.title: Italy: Coopla Green SCoop is already an example of a successful WBO
item.description: This acquisition by the workers, organized as a worker cooperative, demonstrates a viable mechanism to prevent the closure of regional industries and preserve operational knowledge, marking a paradigm shift in the management of business crises in southern Italy.

item.title: Italy and the worker cooperative as a business succession solution
item.description: A seminar in Ravenna focused on transforming businesses into worker cooperatives to maintain and develop companies without successors. A paradigm shift in succession crisis management is taking place across Europe, elevating cooperation to a practical economic policy tool. We are witnessing a wave of new cooperatives formed from existing companies at risk.

item.title: Women-led worker cooperatives show initial results in Türkiye
item.description: Hemdem, a women-led production and business cooperative based in Sultanbeyli, Istanbul, is growing thanks to a project supported by the European Union (EU) and Turkey's Ministry of Industry and Technology. The cooperative brings together local housewives and refugee women to "keep social harmony alive in the kitchen." With flexible working hours and a child-friendly environment, Hemdem employs 45 women and, thanks to an EU grant, acquired a cookie-making machine to increase its production capacity.

item.title: "If we don't produce it, then who will?"
item.description: This Greek article directly challenges the traditional ownership paradigm and underscores the productive autonomy of cooperatives. This challenge is significant in an environment where there is a push to redefine labor relations and the value generated, potentially pointing toward a consolidation or expansion of the cooperative model as a structural alternative in Greece.

item.title: ARTE Documentary: «Italy, from factory to cooperative»
item.description: The dissemination of documentaries, especially through significant media platforms such as ARTE, about the transformation of a factory into a cooperative in Italy, points to a cultural validation and a potential driver of awareness for the cooperative work model as an alternative to traditional business structures in crisis.

item.title: Construction company doubles profits after announcement of move to EOT
item.description: The fact that a company doubled its profits just before the transition is significant, as it combats skepticism about the economic viability of these models. This could encourage other established companies to consider employee transfer as a proactive strategy for optimization and value retention.

item.title: The aerospace sector also resorts to transferring ownership to the workers.
item.description: Space Solutions, UK, announces its transition to employee ownership. The change in ownership at an advanced services company suggests a growing trend in the private sector to retain talent and ensure business continuity through models alternative to traditional sale. Adopting employee ownership structures is increasingly becoming a common succession strategy in mid-sized companies.

item.title: Ireland: Roadshows to promote succession through worker ownership
item.description: Employee Ownership Ireland Roadshows: Ireland is actively promoting employee ownership through "roadshows," a coordinated effort to foster this business model as a viable economic strategy, not just a niche. This initiative in Ireland signals a broader movement in Europe toward the institutionalization of collective ownership models as a response to business succession and the pursuit of greater economic resilience. Active promotion by government bodies indicates that employee ownership is transitioning from a fringe option to a serious economic policy tool.

item.title: What lies beneath the rise of worker ownership in the US
item.description: "According to an independent study, young adults working at companies with ESOPs have an average household net worth 92% higher and salary income 33% higher than their peers at companies without ESOPs. Employees also stay with the company longer: companies with ESOPs have lower turnover, were three to four times more likely to retain staff during COVID-19, and show lower bankruptcy rates during economic downturns."
item.content:encoded: "According to an independent study, young adults working at companies with ESOPs have an average household net worth 92% higher and salary income 33% higher than their peers at companies without ESOPs. Employees also stay with the company longer: companies with ESOPs have lower turnover, were three to four times more likely to retain staff during COVID-19, and show lower bankruptcy rates during economic downturns."

item.title: Those small differences
item.description: A good article for understanding the difference between a "canonical" Worker Cooperative (according to Spanish Law 27/1999) and a French SCOP. We always say that Spanish cooperative legislation is the most advanced in the world, and for good reason.

item.title: Public consultancies to encourage worker-oriented business succession
item.description:
Achieving worker ownership in succession in all types of companies seeking a buyer is already a public policy objective in the #USA.

"The Massachusetts Office of Business Development announced the launch of a statewide center that will help businesses explore and adopt employee-owned models across the state."

The Massachusetts Center for Employee Ownership (MassCEO), which opened on October 1, is a resource for companies interested in employee ownership models to support succession planning, build wealth for workers, promote income equity, and strengthen employee engagement.

item.title: From the fight against illiteracy to soft skills
item.description: Cap Savoir: From Association to Pioneering Worker Cooperative in Training in France. This article presents a success story of a worker cooperative in France, highlighting its evolution from a small association to a pioneering training organization, underscoring the potential of worker cooperatives to generate social impact and sustainable growth in the education sector.

item.title: Bullseye turns 25
item.description: How a college radio program evolved into a successful #EOT and a small media empire, illustrating the potential of worker-owned businesses as a model for sustainable growth in the US.

item.title: Recycling
item.description: A French worker cooperative has launched an advanced recycling platform. This initiative has regional impact, offering a scalable and significant model for the growth of worker-owned businesses.

item.title: #ESOPs and the state budget in #Canada
item.description: This article is significant because it advocates for government policies in Canada to strengthen employee ownership, which could increase the number of worker-owned businesses and their influence on the economy.

item.title: #WorkerCooperative in #Japan
item.description: In Japan, 168 worker cooperatives have been established in 36 prefectures in the three years since the law came into effect.

item.title: From financialization to crypto subordination
item.description: US cryptocurrency companies have set their sights on credit unions. By severing their ties with worker cooperatives, the ICA left them vulnerable to financialization, and from there, it's just a short step to turning them into casinos for crypto mafias.

item.title: Climate planning
item.description: A #WorkerCooperative from #Canada is leading climate planning in North America.

item.title: #ESOPs and #EOTs remain a hot topic in industrial America
item.description: The author advocates for expanding employee ownership in Michigan, #USA, to create a more resilient and equitable economy, highlighting the benefits for workers and the community.

item.title: Lighting cooperative
item.description: Elaul SCOP celebrates 20 years as a worker cooperative. Based in Tarn-et-Garonne, it has established its reputation in the security lighting equipment sector.

item.title: Basque cooperativism loses its way
item.description: The strategic plan for Basque cooperatives talks about narrative, diversity, entrepreneurship... but not about #work, #community or responsibility.

item.title: The Xunta submits to public exhibition the new Law on cooperatives that will promote strategic activities and simplify procedures - Xunta de Galicia
item.description:
New Cooperative Law in Galicia seeks to promote strategic activities and simplify procedures.
https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMi0AFBVV95cUxNNlZ2YVFjS243djJVcVM0OVlrblkxREtQZE02V01NVWFQMU5Zb2R5QzUxbFJWMXJYV216VFNVNko0TmJxMTBOVDJmZjAtZVB2QXVRVXNlWjl5Wk 9QVFlYNjV6VkNaYkxXWGZnYUJILXoyak9xTHFCUXM2bTR2ZV9wTFRfYmFHQUx0QnRLNlhibFZKWVFEOUR aSzRTLXJZX3JwbWd3WVdnMzVzaWhick9MdEoxcVA1OEoyRW5tY0JuZ3FoYVRTV2dvNHdETmhXMHdZ?oc=5
The Galician government has submitted a new cooperatives law for public comment, which aims to promote the cooperative model, boost strategic activities, and simplify administrative procedures, potentially strengthening the growth and influence of worker cooperatives in the region.

item.title: Cooperative drive: Cooperation is the way - cadenaser.com
item.description:
Cooperative drive: Cooperation is the way
https://news.google.com/rss/articles/?oc=5
The article highlights the importance of the cooperative model in Spain, implying growth and strengthening of the sector by presenting it as a viable and relevant alternative.

item.title: #Extremadura presents its strategy for the development of #cooperatives
item.description: The Regional Government of Extremadura presents the Social Economy Strategy for Extremadura for the three-year period 2025-2027.
item.content:encoded: The Regional Government of Extremadura presents the Social Economy Strategy for Extremadura for the three-year period 2025-2027.

item.title: Cooperative carpentry
item.description: Botmeur, #France. A carpentry workshop, a typical example of the new wave of non-technological #WorkCooperatives in #rural areas.
item.content:encoded: Botmeur, #France. A carpentry workshop, a typical example of the new wave of non-technological #WorkCooperatives in #rural areas.

item.title: 130 years of worker cooperativism
item.description: Morlaix, #France. On Friday, September 26, 2025, the Union des couvreurs cooperative of salaried contractors, located in Morlaix, organizes its first open day. It was born from a working society of roofers in 1895.
item.content:encoded: Morlaix, #France. On Friday, September 26, 2025, the Union des couvreurs cooperative of salaried contractors, located in Morlaix, organizes its first open day. It was born from a working society of roofers in 1895.

item.title: From SL labor to S.Coop.
item.description: Sainte-Marie-aux-Mines, #France. On the occasion of its 18th anniversary, "Les Artisans Associés" becomes a #Workers'Cooperative and changes its name to "Enerios Associés" to better reflect its core business: the installation of #photovoltaic panels.

item.title: Fundraising parties to finance the transition to #WorkCooperative
item.description: Former employees of La Vienesa, the famous Montevideo bakery, held a festival. They are seeking to raise funds to establish a #WorkCooperative and reopen.
item.content:encoded: Former employees of La Vienesa, the famous Montevideo bakery, held a festival. They are seeking to raise funds to establish a #WorkCooperative and reopen.

item.title: The typical profile of a Worker Cooperative in France
item.description:
Wlidip celebrates its 20th anniversary as a #WorkerCooperative. It's the typical profile in #France: a #SME with a strong #technology component in a small city that is cooperating and growing.

They started by acquiring the company that employed three people. Now they employ 40 people, and they achieved a turnover of €4.5 million in 2024 and a net profit of 9%.

ISO 27001 and HDS (Health Data Hosting) certified, Widip serves approximately 800 clients, primarily SMEs in the Grenoble and Lyon regions, local governments, and public organizations specializing in health, disability, and child protection.

It specializes in cloud hosting, cybersecurity, outsourcing, technical support, and hardware sales. Founded in 2012, it became a #WorkCooperative in 2015 after being acquired by three employees. The director: "This changes the way we work: no one counts their hours here."

Furthermore, they rely heavily on economies of scope and the power of Open Source and AI tools. "Widip has developed a unified platform, MyWidip, that centralizes contract management, technical action tracking, and incident management. By integrating AI, the solution also enables automated initial diagnosis and the activation of rapid support. Mywidip, which is already used by some of the company's clients, will be launched in 2026. "Previously, we would have needed to create a company and raise funding," explains director Marc Perotto.
item.content:encoded:
Wlidip celebrates its 20th anniversary as a #WorkerCooperative. It's the typical profile in #France: a #SME with a strong #technology component in a small city that is cooperating and growing.

They started by acquiring the company that employed three people. Now they employ 40 people, and they achieved a turnover of €4.5 million in 2024 and a net profit of 9%.

ISO 27001 and HDS (Health Data Hosting) certified, Widip serves approximately 800 clients, primarily SMEs in the Grenoble and Lyon regions, local governments, and public organizations specializing in health, disability, and child protection.

It specializes in cloud hosting, cybersecurity, outsourcing, technical support, and hardware sales. Founded in 2012, it became a #WorkCooperative in 2015 after being acquired by three employees. The director: "This changes the way we work: no one counts their hours here."

Furthermore, they rely heavily on economies of scope and the power of Open Source and AI tools. "Widip has developed a unified platform, MyWidip, that centralizes contract management, technical action tracking, and incident management. By integrating AI, the solution also enables automated initial diagnosis and the activation of rapid support. Mywidip, which is already used by some of the company's clients, will be launched in 2026. "Previously, we would have needed to create a company and raise funding," explains director Marc Perotto.

item.title: Now or never
item.description: The #EnergyCooperative world is moving forward and launching a campaign encouraging the creation of #CEL (Local Energy Communities) in #Euskadi, #CyL, and #Madrid.

item.title: Telex 1.0
item.description: We've just uploaded version 1.0 of Telex to GitHub. This first full version saves an archive of all user-curated content over time, enabling #AI to analyze trends, developments, and inflections by comparing time periods.

item.title: Laure, from Duralex
item.description: Laure, an employee and shareholder of Duralex, now the iconic #Workers'Cooperative in #France, recounts the company's recovery by its own workers after bankruptcy proceedings.

item.title: To understand the #ACI effect
item.description: According to this year's report on the mutual and cooperative sector in Great Britain, cooperatives and mutuals collectively had a turnover of £179.2 billion and employed more than 1.5 million people. However, amidst all this immensity, the Worker's Cooperative represents a mere anecdote. Less than £240 million in combined turnover among the mere 372 existing across the country. This is the ACI effect: the result of making work invisible, promoting managerialism, and focusing on promoting all types of cooperatives that generate financial flows: consumer, health insurance, mutuals, home ownership...

item.title: In the US the movement is not just EOT
item.description: In the #US, the worker-owned business movement isn't just channeled through #EOTs and #ESOPs. "Sol Power Solar," a Rhode Island-based #WorkCooperative for residential photovoltaic installations, is an example of the other side of the movement, taking European cues and standing out for its rejection of #managerialism.

item.title: 55 years old
item.description: "Eighth Day," a Manchester-based #workcooperative that runs vegan shops and cafes, is turning 55. It has a very maximalist, equal pay structure and a consensus-based organizational system. If they gave the Iberian pig a chance, we'd feel much closer to them, honestly.

item.title: The typical #EOT in the #USA
item.description: Miller Engineering is one of the oldest companies in Missouri. Family-controlled by the founder's direct descendants, it has completed thousands of projects and has hundreds of clients. Until now, employees held a 20% stake. Over the next three years, they will hold 100%.
